items.title: Breast cancer survivor finds quality care close to home
items.description: Julie Scholes had it all—love, health, and a job she adored. But in spring 2023, a post-workout chest pain and lump changed everything.

items.title: The importance of sports physicals for youth athletes
items.description: Safeguard your child’s game with sports physicals before the season.

items.title: What’s the Difference Between Probiotics and Prebiotics?
items.description: We know they’re good for us, but what’s the difference and benefits of each?

items.title: What You Need to Know About Your Visit to the ER
items.description: A trip to the Emergency Room is less than ideal, to say the least. Understanding what will happen once you arrive can reduce some of the stress and ease the frustration of not knowing what to expect.

items.title: Better Heart Health Means More Birthdays... and That's a Good Thing
items.description: There's a sort-of consensus out there that once you hit a certain age, you don't really look forward to your birthdays. Especially when you hit 40, 50, 60 or higher. David Larsen, one of the firefighters competing in the 2014 My Heart Challenge, celebrated his 40th birthday this past week

items.title: The Benefits of Recovery – It Does Your Body (AND YOUR GAME) Good
items.description: As the fall sports season comes to a close, some of you may start to think of upcoming tournaments, travel plans, and holidays. For athletes and parents, now is the time to think…recovery.

items.title: 7 Ways to Overcome Depression Without Medication
items.description: The standard medical treatment for depression includes antidepressant medications, such as Lexapro or Zoloft. These medications can be helpful if you’re struggling with depression. However, antidepressants do not always resolve depressive symptoms and can also have unwanted side effects.

items.title: 6 Tips for Interacting Positively with Children on the Autism Spectrum
items.description: Children with autism are sometimes ignored and misunderstood. Learn how to recognize the signs and communicate positively with children with this sometimes subtle disability.
